_comment: Niner knob bake — neutral light-grey matte plastic, flat-top plateau raised above a ledge ring with a soft outer bevel (matches tools/blender/refs/ai_knobs). Renders TWO files: knob_cap.png (lit knob, tinted per-section at runtime via painter.image colour-multiply) and knob_shadow.png (soft contact shadow, drawn untinted under the cap). One bake serves every section colour because matte shading scales linearly with albedo.

canvas._comment: 256 native; runtime scales down to ~18-46 px knobs. scale=4 -> 1024 native gives clean lighting falloff at any GUI scale.

cap._comment: radius_px 110 of the 128 half-canvas -> knob fills 0.859 of frame. Rim body (flat top at rim_height) + a plateau poking up plateau_rise above it, leaving a flat ledge ring from plateau_radius..radius = the inner ring. Bevels soften every top edge.

material._comment: Bright neutral base so runtime tints multiply down without losing highlight detail. roughness 0.40 + a light clearcoat give a defined (but not mirror) highlight so the cap reads crisp at small GUI sizes instead of mushy-matte.

lights._comment: Key upper-left warm, fill lower-right cool, rim grazing the top edge. Same directions as chassis.json so the knob reads as lit by the identical studio rig.

render._comment: scale=5 -> 1280x1280 native; with runtime trilinear mipmaps this keeps the cap crisp even on the big knobs at 2x UI. view_transform Standard (Filmic/AgX would crush blacks and break the edge match).
